channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
item.description: GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to ru...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Exploit Doesn't Exist. You Can Still Prove It Works Against You
item.description: Attackers can now weaponize newly disclosed vulnerabilities far faster than most organizations can patch them. Picus Security explains how security teams can validate exploitability before a public ex...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
item.description: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Why email security teams are drowning in alerts
item.description: Phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks continue to overwhelm security teams with alerts and investigations. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection and response workfl...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
item.description: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT).
The list of identified packages, is below -
aes-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
item.description: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) softwar...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
item.description: OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last mont...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
item.description: An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users in multiple countries with deceptive messages that push VBScript files, leading to remote system access. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
item.description: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading oppor...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
item.description: A newly disclosed FFmpeg flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' could be exploited for remote code execution on Jellyfin servers under certain conditions, and can also trigger a denial-of-service condition in appl...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
item.description: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credent...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code.
"Attack...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft says Windows 11 26H2 is coming soon, details upgrade process
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 version 26H2 will be the next feature update and that devices running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2 will be able to upgrade using a small enablement package. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft fixes AutoGen Studio flaw that enabled code execution
item.description: A vulnerability chain dubbed AutoJack in Microsoft's AutoGen Studio interface for prototyping AI agents could let attackers manipulate an agent into executing arbitrary commands on its host system sim...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
item.description: A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through the sa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to stealthily...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
item.description: Attackers no longer need to sift through massive credential dumps. They can pay others to do it for them. Flare explores how an emerging underground market searches stolen credential databases for spe...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER.
According to Elastic Security Labs, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
item.description:
Google has set September 30, 2026, as the day it begins enforcing Android developer verification in the first four countries, and the major device-maker app stores are in from the start.
On that date...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
item.description: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security progra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
item.description:
It’s Monday again.
This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware asking...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
item.description:
Canada's spy service got a judge's permission to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear sitting on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets.
The Federal Court released a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
item.description: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. QiAnXin's XLab calls it AryStinger and ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
item.description: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized criminal ne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide
item.description: A previously undocumented malware botnet named AryStinger has compromised more than 4,000 outdated routers to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
item.description: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
item.description: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS sco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
item.description: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
